_comment_instructions: Replace BUCKET_NAME with your S3 bucket name (e.g. antiguedad-rds-prod).
Replace CLIENT_ID with the client slug (e.g. networks, hopdesk).
Remove all '_comment_*' keys before pasting into AWS — JSON doesn't support comments.
Attach one copy of this policy to each client's IAM user.

Statement._comment_1: Allow object-level read + write ONLY within this client's prefix.

_comment_2: Allow ListBucket ONLY when the prefix is scoped to this client's folder.
Without this, aws.s3::s3readRDS() fails even with GetObject granted — the SDK
does a HeadObject which internally uses a list call.
The Condition block means: ListBucket is only permitted when the request's
s3:prefix starts with CLIENT_ID/ (i.e. cannot enumerate root or other folders).

_comment_3: Allow read access to hd-admin/ prefix.
Required for: emergency_lock.rds check (every login), username_index collision check
(every user creation), client_registry read (context switcher, invite flow).
This is deliberately read-only — clients never write to hd-admin/.
Future IAM hardening: replace this with a dedicated cross-account role assumption
so client keys cannot even read hd-admin/ — only an explicit STS AssumeRole call
from a specific lambda or the hd-admin app would have access.

_comment_4: Allow ListBucket on hd-admin/ prefix (same reason as ClientPrefixListOnly).
Scoped only to hd-admin/ so a client key cannot enumerate the bucket root.

_comment_5: This is the critical control: without this deny, a user with s3:ListBucket on the
bucket ARN (from statements above) could enumerate the bucket root and discover
Explicit Deny overrides any Allow — even policies attached to the same user.
